title: Do not call Enumerable.Cast\<T> or Enumerable.OfType\<T> with incompatible types
description: Enumerable.Cast\<T> and Enumerable.OfType\<T> require compatible types to function expectedly. The generic cast (IL 'unbox.any') used by the sequence returned by Enumerable.Cast\<T> will throw InvalidCastException at runtime on elements of the types specified. The generic type check (C# 'is' operator/IL 'isinst') used by Enumerable.OfType\<T> will never succeed with elements of types specified, resulting in an empty sequence. Widening and user defined conversions are not supported with generic types.
